provenance_note: Bounded, user-approved CryptoHFTData probe for issue #64: exactly one preregistered hourly object fetched under the request_bounds.probe caps (1 object, <=3 attempts, 120 s/attempt, 512 MiB cumulative fail-closed), then the preregistered offline chd-validate / chd-replay / decide pipeline run under /tmp/jepa-expensive-compute.lock. Only permitted_april_metrics were computed; no raw market rows appear here (aggregate counts, hashes, reason codes, and fixed integrity metrics only). No expansion was requested or performed (expansion_approved_by absent).

replay.detail: window contains no valid snapshot and no carried state

comparison.reason: no CryptoHFTData top-K frame was produced — the fail-closed replay refused (missing_initial_snapshot), so there is nothing to compare against the certified Lake frame

decision.detail: Crypto Lake approved on internal certification only; independent validation not executable (CryptoHFTData window unavailable or refused fail-closed); fallback none

lake_reproducibility.note: the native replay-conformance regenerated the certified perp top-K frame BIT-FOR-BIT versus the index_64 pin, and tick-scale re-derived perp scale 10 / spot 100 under the current preregistration content hash — an independent reproduction of the Lake certification on this branch

interpretation: The probe object is genuine, well-formed Binance-futures BTCUSDT hour-12 L2 data (5,743,827 rows, correct schema/identity, >99.99% in-hour on both time axes). It carries ONLY 'update' events and no snapshot, consistent with CryptoHFTData emitting the L2 snapshot at capture/stream start rather than in every hourly partition (the vendor's documented streaming model; not separately verified here). The preregistered fail-closed replayer therefore refuses to reconstruct hour 12 in isolation (missing_initial_snapshot). Independent cross-source validation is NOT executable from a single mid-day hour and is procedurally blocked: the preregistered request_bounds.expansion gate (enforced by scripts/run_binance_source_gate.py) unlocks more hours ONLY when the probe's own chd-replay is certified, which a delta-only hour cannot satisfy. Reconstructing hour 12 would require some earlier hour to carry a usable snapshot — an UNVERIFIED hypothesis, since only hour 12 was checked here and it was delta-only; no artifact yet proves an earlier hour of this exact product/date contains a snapshot. Obtaining and replaying those hours (binance_futures/2026-04-01 hours 00..12) is a DIFFERENT request than the preregistered expansion and would require a dated, reviewed preregistration amendment (or a separately-scoped bounded request), not just an approval. This outcome does not weaken the Lake certification — the certified perp frame reproduced bit-for-bit — it means the probe alone could not serve as the independent validator, so the preregistered routing is lake_go on internal certification only, fallback none.
